campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 5, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review transportation funding investments for March 2026, adopt a new policy about how the city's property can be used, and discuss ways to recruit and keep city employees. The meeting included a presentation from the regional transit authority about how the city's money is being invested.
keyTopics: Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority investment report for March 2026
New city policy restricting use of city property for federal civil enforcement
City recruitment and retention efforts to fill open positions

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — April 15, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing to address concerns about cleaning up hazardous conditions on certain properties that the County identified. The Council then voted to approve an official order requiring that cleanup work to be done. They also adopted a new city policy on technology disruption and approved a process for residents to request reasonable accommodations.
keyTopics: Public hearing on hazardous property cleanup and abatement orders
New Technology Disruption Policy replacing previous version
Process for requesting reasonable accommodations

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — May 19, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council met on May 19, 2026, to approve financial records, accept donations, and authorize contracts for road paving and other services. The council also moved forward with grant applications and set the town's project priorities for the next fiscal year.
keyTopics: Approved financial report for March 2026
Accepted donation of automated external defibrillator (AED)
Authorized road paving contract with O'Grady Paving
Approved grant applications for statewide programs
Set list of projects for fiscal year 2026-2027

san-jose.title: San José City Council — May 20, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to plan upcoming agendas and review committee work plans. The May 26 meeting was cancelled, so the council focused on preparing the June 2 agenda and discussed updates to neighborhood services and city government procedures.
keyTopics: Cancelled May 26 City Council meeting
June 2, 2026 draft agenda items and scheduling
Neighborhood Services and Education Committee work plan update
Rules and Open Government Committee work plan review
Various city-sponsored events approval

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — May 20, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met on May 20, 2026, to approve meeting minutes and hear public comments from residents. The main focus was selecting and recommending applicants for the Youth Advisory Committee for the 2026-27 fiscal year.
keyTopics: Youth Advisory Committee applicant selection for 2026-27
Public oral communications and resident input
City Council meeting minutes approval
Committee and staff reports

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — May 19, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a regular meeting on May 19, 2026, where they recognized Asian Pacific American Heritage Month and Affordable Housing Month. The council convened with standard opening procedures including the Pledge of Allegiance and reviewed minutes from the previous April meeting.
keyTopics: Asian Pacific American Heritage Month recognition
Affordable Housing Month observance
April meeting minutes approval

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — May 19, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to update the city's long-term health and safety plans through 2040, recognized the Public Works Department for their work on roads, trees, utilities, and sustainability, and celebrated Asian American Pacific Islander Heritage Month.
keyTopics: Update to Health and Safety Element of the General Plan (2015-2040)
Public Works Department recognition during Public Works Week
Asian American Pacific Islander (AAPI) Month celebration
Public Works divisions recognized: streets, sidewalks, trees, transportation, sustainability

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — May 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara held a special meeting of the Station Area Task Force on May 21, 2026, at City Hall to discuss development and planning issues related to station areas in the city.
keyTopics: Station Area Task Force special meeting convened
Meeting held at City Hall Council Chambers (1500 Warburton Avenue) with Zoom option
Compliance with California Brown Act requirements for public meetings

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — May 19, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a meeting on May 19, 2026, where they voted on several land development projects, passed new city rules through emergency ordinances, and appointed a permanent City Manager. The council also heard from residents during public hearings on zoning changes and property assessments.
keyTopics: Appointment of permanent City Manager
Emergency ordinance to add new city regulations
Site development permit and map amendments
Property assessment confirmation and ordering

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — May 15, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council heard an update on Palo Alto's residential electrification efforts, which aim to help the region meet air quality standards. The council discussed proposed principles for programs that would help residents switch from gas to electric appliances and heating systems.
keyTopics: Residential electrification status update and progress
Proposed principles to guide electrification assistance programs
Compliance with air district regulations
Staff presentation on electrification initiatives
